name: temple-nave
summary: The public worship space of a temple. Long processional nave, pews facing the altar, apse behind. Scale makes worshippers feel small.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: 3:1 very elongated — the nave IS the long axis

accent.purpose: dais / chancel area at the altar end

primary_palette.note: deity effigy as the primary; altar secondary

story_prompts: service in progress — pews half-occupied, censer smoke in the air
dawn, empty — single candle lit at the altar, keeper sweeping
desecrated — altar cloth torn, candles snapped, effigy defaced
threshold of a major feast — extra candles, flowers, ceremonial fabric unrolled

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim reverent (0.3-0.4) — candle and brazier dominant

anti_patterns: altar goes at one end (usually north) — never center
pews in rows with a central aisle, facing the altar
bilateral symmetry across the nave axis — it's a ceremonial space
